formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): The patient’s presentation aligns with Graves’ disease complicated by ophthalmopathy. Treatment with radioactive iodine (RAI) can transiently worsen ocular symptoms due to an immuno-reactive surge triggered by rapid decline in thyroid hormone levels, even though it ultimately resolves hyperthyroidism.

The most appropriate choice reflecting clinically recognized risk is therefore:

\boxed{\text{D}}. Answer choice: D
